phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The plan is persisted.

## Summary

**Story #7 — "Split client name into firstName and lastName"** → [issue #7](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-47a00acd/issues/7), labeled `agent::ready` (wave 0).

**Shape:** One Story (default-single). The four requirements are one cohesive cutover of the client name model — they can't be reviewed or reverted independently — so they're captured as `## Slicing` checkpoints rather than sibling tickets.

**What it binds (acceptance criteria):**
- Create/update accept `firstName`/`lastName`; responses include both.
- Legacy single-`name` writes still work, split by the rule: `"Ada Lovelace"` → `Ada` / `Lovelace`; `"Mary Anne Evans"` → `Mary Anne` / `Evans` (everything before the last space); `"Cher"` → `""` / `Cher` (single word is just the last name).
- Startup migration splits existing `name`-only records in place, and is idempotent across restarts.
- The receivables report (and any other name read surface) shows the full name joined with a single space, with no leading space when first name is empty.

**Design choices recorded in the Spec:** a single shared split + `fullName` helper drives both the write back-compat and the migration (one source of truth); explicit `firstName`/`lastName` win over a co-sent legacy `name`; existing authz is preserved.

**Notable observation:** this checkout has no application source (`git ls-files` = `package.json`, `README.md`, `.gitignore` only; `fileCount: 0` from the codebase snapshot). I recorded that as a verified "Current state" note and marked `changes[]` as advisory — the executing agent must locate the real client model/routes/store/server/report at delivery time and map the changes onto them. The validator auto-normalized the paths to `creates` accordingly.

**Gates:** critics (consolidation, pre-mortem) both skipped for a 1-Story draft; text-hygiene clean; dry-run pre-pass ran warning-clean before the real persist. Gate #1 and Gate #2 auto-proceeded under `--yes`.

Per instructions, I ran **only** the planning pipeline and did not deliver. To execute it: `/deliver 7`.

Delivered. Story #7 — the split-client-names change request — is fully landed on `main`.

## Outcome

**`/deliver 7 --yes` → status: `landed`** (PR #8 merged, Issue #7 closed at `agent::done`).

I discovered the plan the pipeline had opened — a single ready Story (#7), which is the whole "split client names" change request (the v2 model is Story-only, so this is the "Epic" to deliver). I did **not** re-plan; I delivered the existing Spec through the one delivery engine: `single-story-init` → implement in worktree → acceptance self-eval (7/7 AC met) → `single-story-close` → PR → merge to `main`.

The checkout had no application source, so the modules were built from scratch per the Spec's advisory sketch:

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/clients/client-name.js` | Single source of truth: `splitName` (last word = lastName, everything before the last space = firstName, single word → empty firstName) + `fullName` (space-joined, no leading space) |
| `src/clients/client-store.js` | Persistence, write-path name resolution (explicit `firstName`/`lastName` beat co-sent legacy `name`), idempotent in-place `migrate()` |
| `src/clients/clients-routes.js` | Transport-agnostic create/update/read/list + receivables router |
| `src/server.js` | `node:http` bootstrap that runs the migration **before serving traffic** |
| `src/reports/receivables.js` | Receivables report rendering `fullName` (grep-confirmed as the only name-display surface) |
| 4 test files | 29 tests covering AC-1..AC-7 incl. a live-socket round-trip |

**Verification on merged `main`:** full suite `29 pass / 0 fail`, typecheck clean (9 files). Local `main` fast-forwarded to `58f9709` (the base fast-forward that the delivery tail skipped due to the pre-existing dirty `temp/`).

Being a single-Story run, the per-run epilogue (cross-Story audit roster / follow-up rollup / sibling coherence) is intentionally skipped. Nothing is left pending or blocked.
